title: Eenhana

infobox.value: Jan | Feb | Mar | Apr | May | Jun | Jul | Aug | Sep | Oct | Nov | Dec | Year

([Kuanyama](/wiki/Kuanyama_language "Kuanyama language"): *calves*, [IPA](/wiki/International_Phonetic_Alphabet "International Phonetic Alphabet"): [ɛːˈn̥ana]) is the capital town of the [Ohangwena Region](/wiki/Ohangwena_Region "Ohangwena Region"), northern [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"), on the border with [Angola](/wiki/Angola "Angola"). It also used to be a [mission station](/wiki/Mission_(station) "Mission (station)") of the [Finnish Missionary Society](/wiki/Finnish_Missionary_Society "Finnish Missionary Society"). It had a population of 16,588 people in 2023.
[Eenhana Town Council](http://www.eenhanatc.org.na/about-us.aspx)[*[permanent dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*] About us

**Eenhana** ([Kuanyama](/wiki/Kuanyama_language "Kuanyama language"): *calves*, [IPA](/wiki/International_Phonetic_Alphabet "International Phonetic Alphabet"): [ɛːˈn̥ana]) is the capital town of the [Ohangwena Region](/wiki/Ohangwena_Region "Ohangwena Region"), northern [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"), on the border with [Angola](/wiki/Angola "Angola"). It also used to be a [mission station](/wiki/Mission_(station) "Mission (station)") of the [Finnish Missionary Society](/wiki/Finnish_Missionary_Society "Finnish Missionary Society"). It had a population of 16,588 people in 2023.

Eenhana is situated in a [subtropical](/wiki/Subtropics "Subtropics") forest. It is connected to the road network and has a well-developed infrastructure. Due to the proximity of Angola, many businesses are situated here. The town hosts an annual trade fair.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eenhana&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Eenhana was founded around [New Year's Day](/wiki/New_Year%27s_Day "New Year's Day") 1930 by [the Reverend](/wiki/Pastor "Pastor") [Paulus Hamutenya](/wiki/Paulus_Hamutenya "Paulus Hamutenya"). He was one of the first seven [Ovambos](/wiki/Owambo_people "Owambo people") to be [ordained](/wiki/Ordination "Ordination") [pastors](/wiki/Pastor "Pastor") in [Oniipa](/wiki/Oniipa "Oniipa"), [Ovamboland](/wiki/Ovamboland "Ovamboland"), in 1925 by the director of the [Finnish Missionary Society](/wiki/Finnish_Missionary_Society "Finnish Missionary Society"), [Matti Tarkkanen](/w/index.php?title=Matti_Tarkkanen&action=edit&redlink=1 "Matti Tarkkanen (page does not exist)") [[fi](https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matti_Tarkkanen "fi:Matti Tarkkanen")].[[2]](#cite_note-2) The name *Eenhana* comes from the word *calves* in [Oshikwanyama](/wiki/Oshikwanyama "Oshikwanyama") and is a reference to the calves that used to water at the small water pan where Eenhana is now located.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Hamutenya had earlier lived in [Edundja](/wiki/Edundja "Edundja"), where he had built a church. However, the area became crowded, and he decided to found a new settlement for the [Oukwanyama](/wiki/Oukwanyama "Oukwanyama") people in the woods, 60 kilometres (37 mi) to the east of [Engela](/wiki/Engela "Engela"). Ovakwanyamas from Angola had already begun to move in that area. The [South West African](/wiki/South_West_Africa "South West Africa") government made Hamutenya the local head of the tribesmen of Eastern Oukwanyama. In 1932, Hamutenya built a church in Eenhana. He died the same year after a short illness.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The Eenhana [mission station](/wiki/Mission_(station) "Mission (station)") was founded by the nurse [Linda Helenius](/wiki/Linda_Helenius "Linda Helenius") in 1936. Eenhana thus became the centre of medical care in Eastern Oukwanyama for several decades. Teacher Suoma Hirvonen soon joined Helenius on this new mission station.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Before Namibian independence it was a military centre of the [South African Defence Force](/wiki/South_African_Defence_Force "South African Defence Force") without public infrastructure. It was proclaimed a settlement in 1992, and a town in 1999.

## Economy and infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eenhana&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy and infrastructure")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/Eenhana_Open_Market.jpg/250px-Eenhana_Open_Market.jpg)](/wiki/File:Eenhana_Open_Market.jpg)

The entrance to Eenhana Open Market (circa 2013). This market no longer exists.

The town attempts to attract businesses to the town through business exhibitions, small and medium enterprises and tourism activities. Eenhana hosts two annual trade fairs, The Council-run Eenhana Trade and Business Expo since 2008, and the Eenhana Annual Youth Festival, inaugurated in 2019.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The Town Council is investing into marketing activities directed at investors and is assisting business development by developing open markets and providing business skills.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eenhana&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education")]

Eenhana is the home to six schools, three primary, one special school and two secondary:

* Eenhana Primary School
* Paulus Hamuntenya Primary School
* Haimbili Haufiku Secondary School
* [Usko Nghaamwa](/wiki/Usko_Nghaamwa "Usko Nghaamwa") Special School
* Eenhana Secondary School
* Nanhapo Primary School (the most recent of these schools)

Other education institutions include:

* The [University of Namibia](/wiki/University_of_Namibia "University of Namibia")'s Centre for External Studies
* Tate Institute of Technology
* Glowdom Educational Foundation
* [NamCOL](/w/index.php?title=NamCOL&action=edit&redlink=1 "NamCOL (page does not exist)") Centre
* [Eenhana Vocational Training Centre](/w/index.php?title=Eenhana_Vocational_Training_Centre&action=edit&redlink=1 "Eenhana Vocational Training Centre (page does not exist)")

On the 10th March 2022, the [Namibia University of Science and Technology](/wiki/Namibia_University_of_Science_and_Technology "Namibia University of Science and Technology") (NUST) opened its satellite campus at Eenhana. The campus was officially opened by Prime Minister Saara Kuugongelwa - Amadhila.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On the 11 March 2025, the 5th [International University of Management](/wiki/International_University_of_Management "International University of Management") (IUM) campus was officially inaugurated in Eenhana. The campus was officially opened by the founder of IUM Professor [David Namwandi](/wiki/David_Namwandi "David Namwandi"). The campus is located along the Dr Sam Nujoma Road in Eenhana town.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eenhana&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Politics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/07/A_Main_Street_in_Eenhana_.jpg/250px-A_Main_Street_in_Eenhana_.jpg)](/wiki/File:A_Main_Street_in_Eenhana_.jpg)

A Main Street in Eenhana

Eenhana is governed by a town council that has seven seats.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The [2015 local authority election](/wiki/Namibian_local_and_regional_elections,_2015 "Namibian local and regional elections, 2015") was won by [SWAPO](/wiki/SWAPO "SWAPO") which gained six seats (1,322 votes). The remaining seat was won by the [Rally for Democracy and Progress](/wiki/Rally_for_Democracy_and_Progress_(Namibia) "Rally for Democracy and Progress (Namibia)") (RDP) with 152 votes.[[13]](#cite_note-13) SWAPO also won the [2020 local authority election](/wiki/2020_Namibian_local_and_regional_elections "2020 Namibian local and regional elections"). It obtained 1,182 votes and gained five seats. The [Independent Patriots for Change](/wiki/Independent_Patriots_for_Change "Independent Patriots for Change") (IPC), an opposition party formed in August 2020, obtained 402 votes and gained the remaining two seats.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

[Electoral Commission of Namibia](/wiki/Electoral_Commission_of_Namibia "Electoral Commission of Namibia"). 29 November 2020. p. 9. Archived from [the original](https://www.ecn.na/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/Media-Release-on-LA-Results_2020.pdf) (PDF) on 24 January 2021. Retrieved 25 January 2021.
